title: Question of Kamčev, Liebenau, Wood and Yepremyan on planar graphs
statement_text: What are general bounds on the size-Ramsey numbers of bounded-degree planar graphs?
context_text: Kamčev, Liebenau, Wood and Yepremyan [21] asked for general bounds for bounded-degree planar graphs. Prior to this work, only the grid graph (bound $O(n^{3/2})$, recently improved to $O(n^{5/4})$ by Conlon, Nenadov and Trujić), cycles, and bounded-degree trees (linear bounds) had been studied in this class.
paper_contribution: Proves that for every proper minor-closed class $\mathcal{G}$ and every $n$-vertex graph $G \in \mathcal{G}$ of maximum degree $\Delta$, $\hat{r}_k(G) = \tilde{O}(n^{3/2 - \frac{1}{2\Delta}})$, which in particular applies to planar graphs, thereby answering this question.
notes: The question is described in prose in the introduction and abstract with citation [21]; no labeled theorem environment. The paper explicitly states it addresses this question.
paper_title: Size-Ramsey numbers of structurally sparse graphs
